drawings.description: Four circular sigils arranged in a 2x2 grid. Each sigil has a circular border, a central vertical structure resembling a pillar or staff with a sphere/head on top, and contains various symbols, figures, and marks.

details.sigil_top_left: Circle with central pillar topped by a small figure/face with antennae. Top has radial lines/sun rays. Two small circles with eyes/faces in upper area. Lower section shows human-like figures on either side of the pillar base, with small circles.
details.sigil_top_right: Circle with central pillar, top has a face/head symbol. Upper section has wavy water-like lines and a sun symbol. Middle-right has a circle with cross/plus inside. Lower-left has an animal figure (possibly a goat or beast).
details.sigil_bottom_left: Circle with central pillar topped by a face/head with circles on sides. Horizontal line with hash marks. Lower section shows human-like figures on either side, with several circles on the right side.
details.sigil_bottom_right: Circle with central pillar, top has face symbol and wavy lines. Lower section has two square panels with figures inside, each panel containing a cross/plus symbol and human figures.

mixed_media_regions.description: Main text block in esoteric/constructed script occupying the upper two-thirds of the page. The script contains a mix of alphabetic-like characters, punctuation, and unusual symbols that do not match any known natural language.
Four ceremonial/magical sigils that combine text-like markings, symbolic figures (humans, animals, celestial symbols), and structural elements (pillars, circles, crosses).

uncertain: The script used in the text blocks cannot be identified as any known natural or constructed language - it appears to be a fictional/ceremonial script, possibly from a magical or esoteric grimoire tradition
Characters that resemble 'Q', 'H', 'X', 'Y', 'J', 'T', 'E', 'I', 'B', '8', '0', '<', '>', '[', ']', '*', '+' may be transcription artifacts or actual script elements
The exact meaning and identity of the four sigils is unclear - they appear to be ceremonial magic seals possibly from a fictional magical system
Whether the script is an original invention or based on an existing cipher/alphabet cannot be determined from the image alone
